Dear Jeanette:
Every two years my office updates and publishes the University's
I'PoIiticaI Reform Act Disqualification Requirementsrr pamphlet
(enclosed) to be distributed to a1l- University ernployees. We are
scheduling a newly revised edition to be published and dis-
tributed in June, 1990.
Other than updating the Coordinatorsr names and addresses, the
only substantive change we determined was the reference to the
material financial effect regulations effective November, L988
(L8702 through 1-8702.5). We would appreciate your comments
regarding any additional revisions that may need to be brought to
our attention.
Enclosed for your review is a copy of the pamphlet with our
proposed changes in red ink. For reference, the numbers in blue
ink refer to the appropriate Sections of the Code and Regu1a-
tions.

sections.question_synthetic: The University of California is requesting FPPC review and comments on a revised Political Reform Act Disqualification Requirements pamphlet scheduled for publication in June 1990, specifically seeking feedback on updates to material financial effect regulations and other substantive changes.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response/advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present. Document requests feedback within a two-week timeframe. The letter includes a time constraint for publishing by June 1990.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The University of California is requesting FPPC review and comments on a revised Political Reform Act Disqualification Requirements pamphlet scheduled for publication in June 1990, specifically seeking feedback on updates to material financial effect regulations and other substantive changes.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from UC Berkeley's Office of the President seeking FPPC comments on a revised Political Reform Act Disqualification Requirements pamphlet before publication in June 1990, with proposed changes marked in red ink.
